title: eriogonum deflexum subsp. parryi
intro: Eriogonum deflexum subsp. parryi, also known as Parry's buckwheat, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to the southwestern United States. It belongs to the Polygonaceae family and is commonly found in dry and sandy areas. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Eriogonum deflexum subsp. parryi include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and protecting against oxidative stress, and it can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for medicinal use.
